issues.detail: Translation is null. Suggested: "A certain port in South Vietnam was reportedly successfully defended against enemy frogmen by dolphins that had been taught to kill."

Translation is garbled text from another line of the poem ("Und was er braucht..."). The text is archaic German: "Hier geb ich ihm zwey Stübchen ein" = "Here I give him two small rooms". Full context needed for proper translation.
All annotations are from English translation text ("The", "food", "must", "be") instead of German source text. Should annotate: Gargut, Oberfläche (noun), Fett (noun), spritzt (verb/spritzen).
No annotations present. Should have: spritzen (verb, gloss_hint "dilute"), Wein (noun), Leitungswasser (noun).
Annotation for "Spritz" has gloss_hint "spray" but the sense is "ejaculate" (sense 7). gloss_hint should be "ejaculate".
Translation contains HTML span tags instead of plain text. Translation should be plain: "They blasted a large block out of the rock." Also no annotations array present.
No annotations present. Should have: Lumumba (noun), Blöcke (noun/Block, gloss_hint "bloc (political)"), Kalten (adjective/kalt), Krieges (noun/Krieg).
No annotations present. Should have: Blocks (noun/Block, gloss_hint "housing block").
No annotations present. Should have: Übeltäter (noun), Block (noun/Block, gloss_hint "clamp block").
"geschoren werden" in nautical context means "reeved" (threaded through), not "cut". Translation should be: "...the Barberholer line can also be reeved directly through shackles or carabiners."
Annotation for Block has gloss_hint "block" which doesn't substring-match gloss_en "pulley block". Should be "pulley block" or "pulley".
Translation says "pad" for Block but this is sense 7 (stamp block/Briefmarkenblock). Should be: "She traded with him and gave him the entire stamp block for the unique stamp."
Annotation for Block has gloss_hint "pad" but should be "stamp block" (sense 7).
Annotation for Block has gloss_hint "block (solid piece)" but should be "block structure" (sense 8).
Annotation for Block has gloss_hint "block" which doesn't clearly match "plant block" (sense 9). Should be "plant block".
Translation says "shooter" but context (bow/Bogen) indicates marksman sense. gloss_hint "shooter" should be "marksman" (sense 0). Also "spannt" gloss_hint "pulls" — more like "draws" for a bow.

Annotation for Schütze has gloss_hint "shooter" but this is the military rank sense (sense 2). Should be "lance corporal". Also translation says "private" which is more accurate than "lance corporal" for Bundeswehr Schütze rank.
"Shooter Meier reports for duty!" — Schütze here is the military rank (sense 3: rifleman), not "shooter". Should be: "Private Meier reports for duty!"
Annotation for Schütze has gloss_hint "shooter" but should be "rifleman" (sense 3).
Annotation for "Schütze" shows gloss_hint missing in grep output but translation says "shooter Borek" — should be "private/rifleman Borek" (sense 3). Translation should say "Private Borek".
"the shooter Borek" should be "Private Borek" — Schütze is used as a military rank here (sense 3: rifleman).
No annotations present. Should have: Schützen (noun/Schütze, gloss_hint "rifleman"), waschechte (adjective), Halunken (noun/Halunke).
Annotation for Schütze has gloss_hint "marksman" but this is the weaving shuttle sense (sense 7). Should be "shuttle".

"The marksman threads the weft through the compartment" — Schütze here means shuttle (weaving). Should be: "The shuttle carries the weft thread through the shed." (Fach in weaving = shed, not compartment).
Translation is null. Suggested: "In 1616, Gustavus Selenus listed the chess pieces in the first German chess book and mentioned the Schütze (bishop) alongside the King and Queen."
Annotation for "Sie" has pos "noun" — should be "pronoun". gloss_hint "she (pronoun)" is inconsistent with pos "noun".
Annotation for Vorlage has gloss_hint "template" but this example is about an original/model (sense 1). Should be "original".
"The template for the drawing exercise" — Vorlage here means original/model (sense 1). Should be: "The original for the drawing exercise was a genuine etching by Goya."
Annotation for Vorlage has gloss_hint "template" but this is about a legislative draft (sense 5). Should be "draft".
"The template must still be conclusively dealt with" — Vorlage here means draft/bill (sense 5). Should be: "The draft must still be conclusively dealt with before it can be put to a vote."
Annotation for Vorlage has gloss_hint "template" but this is about forward body lean (sense 9). Should be "forward shift".

"The sensitive balancing of the template" — Vorlage here means forward lean/shift (sense 9, skiing context). Should be: "The sensitive balancing of the forward lean is the key to controlling the speed."
"Have you already covered the bedspreads?" — abdecken here means remove cover (sense 0). Should be: "Have you already removed the bedspreads?"
All annotations are from English text ("The", "storm", etc.) instead of German. Should annotate: Sturm (noun), Haus (noun).
"The storm covered the entire house" — abdecken here means strip the roof (sense 1). Should be: "The storm stripped the entire house (of its roof)."
"The table in the corner still needs to be covered" — abdecken here means clear the table (sense 2). Should be: "The table in the corner still needs to be cleared."
"This horse would have been covered" — abdecken here means process/skin a carcass (sense 3, archaic). Should be: "In earlier times, this horse would have been processed (skinned/rendered)."
"Many diligent hands covered the game" — abdecken here means skin game animals (sense 4, hunting). Should be: "Many diligent hands skinned the game in no time."
Annotations use English lemmas: "manure" for Mist, "the" for der, "knot" for Knoten. Should use German lemmas: Mist, der, Knoten.
Annotations are from English translation text ("go", "step", "once"). Should annotate German: gehen (verb), Schritt (noun), durchgehen (verb).

"This bill will never, ever add up!" — Rechnung here more naturally means calculation/plan, not bill. Better: "This calculation will never, ever work out!" The sense is 'succeed/work out' (sense 6), not strictly 'add up' (sense 5).
"After the bankruptcy, the company went into another (company)" — awkward. Better: "After the bankruptcy, the company was absorbed into another one."
"a master plan doesn't work in the Baltic region" — should say "doesn't work out" to match the aufgehen sense (become clear/succeed).
"During mating season, roses are particularly red" — but this is the wattle sense (sense 3: bare red areas on roosters). Should be: "During mating season, the wattles are particularly red."

Annotation for Rose has gloss_hint "rose (plant)" but this example is used for both erysipelas (sense 6) and shingles (sense 7) senses. The text 'Meine Rose' in context likely refers to the disease, not the plant. gloss_hint should be "erysipelas" or "shingles".
Annotations use English lemmas: "wheat" for Weizen, "pile" for Haufen, "shovel" for schaufeln, "two" for zwei. Should use German lemmas: Weizen, Haufen, Schaufel/schaufeln, zwei.
No annotations present. Should have: Kinderbuchfigur (noun), Conni (noun/name), beliebte (adjective/beliebt), Vorlage (noun, gloss_hint "original"), Memes (noun).
"template for memes" — Vorlage here means source/original (sense 1). Better: "The children's book character Conni is a popular model for memes."
No annotations present. Should have: Wiktionary (noun), Vorlagen (noun/Vorlage, gloss_hint "template"), Wortarten (noun).
No annotations present. Should have: Stürmer (noun), herrliche (adjective), Vorlage (noun, gloss_hint "assist").
No annotations present. Should have: Vorlage (noun, gloss_hint "forward shift"), Rücklage (noun), Sparkassenbeamter (noun).
